stories.headline: Goldman Warns on Copper as Iran War Threatens Global Economy
stories.summary: Copper is vulnerable to further declines if the Strait of Hormuz remains blocked, Goldman Sachs Group Inc. warned, as metals markets brace for President Donald Trump’s deadline for an Iran deal.

stories.headline: Trump Says Iran Could ‘Die Tonight’ as Hormuz Deadline Looms
stories.summary:
- President Donald Trump threatened Iran with civilizational destruction as the US attacked military targets on the country’s key oil export hub, ramping up pressure on Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz by his Tuesday deadline or face massive strikes on critical infrastructure.
Iran halted participation in ceasefire talks in response to Trump’s social media post, according to a person familiar with the matter who asked not to be identified di

stories.headline: US counts cost of equipment destroyed in Iran war

stories.headline: Trump Threatens Broad Attacks in Iran. How Is Illegal Conduct in War Defined?
stories.summary: It is illegal for any military to target civilians, as President Trump has suggested he would in threats against Iran. But the U.S. has sought significant leeway in defining a civilian target.

stories.headline: How Trump Took the U.S. to War With Iran
stories.summary: In a series of Situation Room meetings, President Trump weighed his instincts against the deep concerns of his vice president and a pessimistic intelligence assessment. Here’s the inside story of how he made the fateful decision.

stories.headline: Vance says he prays U.S. is ‘on God’s side’ in Iran war

stories.headline: How serious will the jet fuel crisis in Europe become?
stories.summary: Jet fuel prices have increased by 95% since the United States and Israel launched military attacks against Iran on 28 February. Higher fares, fuel surcharges and reductions in capacity or limiting of unprofitable routes will be the new normal, energy analysts warn.

stories.headline: Bill Ackman proposes €56 billion acquisition of Universal Music Group
stories.summary: Billionaire investor Bill Ackman has proposed a €56 billion deal to merge Universal Music Group with a US acquisition vehicle, delivering shareholders a 78% premium and a potential New York Stock Exchange listing.

stories.headline: US Vice President Vance attacks Brussels and vows to help Orbán ahead of Hungarian vote
stories.summary: Vance accused the European Union of electoral interference in Hungary’s election campaign during a visit to Budapest. Speaking alongside Orbán, Vance described the Hungarian leader as a “role model” for Europe.

stories.headline: Haass: Trump’s Iran Goals More Optimistic Than Realistic

stories.headline: Chicago Atlantic Targets Emerging Markets in Private Credit Push
stories.summary: Chicago Atlantic is moving into private credit markets in the developing world, seeking to seize on increased demand as investors pull money out of similar funds in the US.

stories.headline: How Great Leaders Can Drive Big Change
stories.summary: Persistence separates great leaders from everyone else according to Matt McKnight, CEO of Perimeter. He joined Bloomberg Open Interest to talk about why the most impactful leaders choose problems that seem unsolvable and stay with them anyway. He says you will hear no, that will not work or we have tried that before, but if you understand your domain, think from first principles, and bring others along, you can change entire industries. stories.headline: Eldorado Gold Wins Investor Support to Buy Copper-Focused Foran
stories.summary: Eldorado Gold Corp.’s C$3.8 billion ($2.7 billion) bid to buy Foran Mining Corp. won shareholder support, advancing plans to create a larger Canadian gold-and-copper miner after the company overcame an activist campaign that had cast doubt on the deal’s prospects.

stories.headline: Private Credit Exodus Turns Moody’s BDC Outlook to Negative
stories.summary: A swelling wave of redemptions has driven Moody’s Ratings to revise its outlook for private credit investment vehicles to negative, after holding the line at stable for over two years.

stories.headline: Pimco Weighs $14 Billion Debt Deal for Oracle Data Center
stories.summary: Pacific Investment Management Co. is in talks with Bank of America Corp. to help provide roughly $14 billion of debt financing to build a massive Oracle Corp. data center in Michigan, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: FDIC Lays Out Guidelines for Institutions Issuing Stablecoins
stories.summary: The Federal Deposit Insurance Corp., which backs deposits at thousands of US banks, is laying out its guidelines for how those institutions and their fintech subsidiaries can use stablecoins as digital currencies become more widely accepted in the financial system.

stories.headline: Only Trump Knows Why Bondi Was Fired as Attorney General, Blanche Says
stories.summary: In his first news conference since being elevated to acting attorney general, Todd Blanche said that “nobody has any idea” what led to Pam Bondi’s dismissal other than President Trump.

stories.headline: ‘Definitely a Sham’: As Tariffs Climb, Trade Fraud and Accounting Tricks Proliferate
stories.summary: U.S. imports from China have shrunk drastically. But billions of dollars of the change appear to be the result of accounting gimmicks and outright fraud.

stories.headline: Fact-Checking Republicans’ Misleading Claims About Mail-In Ballots and Fraudulent Voting
stories.summary: President Trump, his administration and G.O.P. lawmakers have claimed widespread issues with mailed ballots and fraudulent voting, but the evidence doesn’t support them.
